content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • •     Administration • •     Executive/Legal • •     Enforcement
(916) 322-5662                322-5660                322-5901                322-4441


September 13, 1984


Natalie E. West
Berkeley City Attorney
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, CA 94704

                                                                Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-323

Dear Natalie:

     I was pleased with our meeting on August 29, and I hope that
we will have more such meetings in the future. As you know, we
are still working on drafts of Regulations 18700, 18701 and
18702, and it is likely to be some time before the Commission
formally resolves the issues of interpreting what it means to
use one's official position to influence a governmental
decision. In the meantime, we are advising that when an
official who sits on an agency disqualifies himself or herself
from a decision because of a financial interest the official
must not:

     (a) Represent any other person with respect to that
decision before the agency, or any other agency which it
controls as set forth in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(e);

     (b) Communicate either orally or in writing with any
other member of such an agency with respect to the decision.

     Pending action by the Commission on a new or amended
regulation on this issue, an official will not be deemed to be
using his or her official position to influence a decision
solely because the official advises the party to be affected by
the decision, prepares plans or other documents which will be
used by the party or the party's representative in connection
with the decision, or communicates with members of the public or
the media respecting the decision on behalf of the party.

Natalie E. West
September 13, 1984
Page 2


     When we have a draft of our proposed regulation we will send
it to your for comments by your Committee.  In the meantime, you
may want to distribute copies of this letter to the members of
the Committee.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:plh

sections.conclusion: When an official disqualifies due to financial interest, the official must not: (a) represent any other person regarding that decision before the agency, or (b) communicate orally or in writing with other agency members about the decision. However, pending new regulations, the official will not be deemed to use their position to influence the decision solely because they advise the affected party, prepare documents, or communicate with the public/media about the decision.
sections.question_synthetic: What conduct is prohibited and what conduct is permitted for a public official who disqualifies themselves from an agency decision due to a financial interest?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that disqualified officials must refrain from representing others or communicating with agency members about the decision. However, pending regulatory amendments, advising the affected party, preparing documents, or communicating publicly about the matter on behalf of the party is permissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers, but content clearly contains both elements structured as advice guidance. Document references prior meeting and ongoing regulatory work. Signature partially illegible but identified as Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What conduct is prohibited and what conduct is permitted for a public official who disqualifies themselves from an agency decision due to a financial interest?

CONCLUSION: When an official disqualifies due to financial interest, the official must not: (a) represent any other person regarding that decision before the agency, or (b) communicate orally or in writing with other agency members about the decision. However, pending new regulations, the official will not be deemed to use their position to influence the decision solely because they advise the affected party, prepare documents, or communicate with the public/media about the decision.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Barbara Milman provides guidance to Berkeley City Attorney regarding prohibited and permitted conduct for public officials who disqualify themselves from decisions due to financial conflicts of interest, pending new Commission regulations on the issue.
